observations.recommendation:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize properties that did not meet the capitalization threshold and adjust affected accounts retrospectively and maintain Semi-Expandable Property Ledger Cards; and

Require the General Services Office to prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and reports mentioned in Section 4.7 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen control and safeguards over semi-expendable properties.

Inc. and Municipal Treasurer’s League of Mis. Occ. Inc. and strictly comply with the rules on procurement as provided for in R.A. No. 9184 in all future similar procurement activities.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal accountant to:

Attach necessary documents required to support the cash advance granted;

Refrain from granting additional cash advances, unless previous ones have been liquidated; and

Monitor and demand the immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advance. 

Direct the Disbursing Officer to:

Refrain from transferring cash advances from one Accountable Officer to another; and

Strictly enforce the period of liquidation as prescribed above and require the immediate refund/return of any excess amount thereof.

We recommended that management refrain from conducting LGU-led trainings, planning conferences and other similar activities outside the locality unless extremely necessary, in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.

We recommended that management properly assess the accountability of accountable officers so that the applied bond will cover even prospective accountability. If feasible, management may assign to other accountable officers the excess of accountability for which a separate Fidelity Bond shall be secured, as provided for in Section 5.1, Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019.

We recommended that Management:

Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to comply the provisions as mandated under Section 254 in the Local Government Code for the posting requirement of the Notice of Delinquency as a condition sine qua non on availing judicial or administrative remedies which the Provincial Treasury Office may pursue;  

Conduct extensive tax campaigns so that taxpayers are apprised of their rights and obligations incidental to their ownership of real properties including consequences on non-payment of RPT/SET; and

Prepare aging schedule of current and prior years’ receivables to easily identify inactive or non-moving in the books of accounts.

We recommendedin December 7, 2020 that management:
Instruct the GSO to prepare a complete inventory report accounting the undistributed welfare goods and explain the reasons for the variances cited;
Organize a team which may be composed of the GSO, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and barangay officials concerned to verify inconsistencies in the signatures of the recipients, and invite this Commission to serve as witness during the validation.
We appreciate the prompt action by management which led to the creation of the validation team. Consequently, the verification made on January- February 2021 unearthed the following reason for the absence and differences in the affixed signatures:
There were two different lines to claim for the cash and non-cash assistance and one has to line-up starting from the end for each separate line. Thus, beneficiaries who have no time and patience for the undue delay were inclined to have theirnon-cash assistance received by the barangay captain or their other relatives.
Some recipients used initials (shortened signatures) in signing the distribution list
Notwithstanding the inconsistencies in the signatures, we found out that 78 out of 80listed beneficiaries cited in this audit finding have received in actual both the cash and non-cash assistance. Two (2) beneficiaries however claimed that they have not received the relief package. The signatures affixed beside their names were from replacement-beneficiaries and the same correction were not effected in both the payroll and distribution list.
In light of the foregoing, we updated our recommendation that management:
Improve the distribution process for future aids that will be extended to avoid unnecessary delays in the receipt of relief packages by the constituents;
Ensure that the distribution lists are titled to serve as confirmation on the part of the constituents on the items distributed and received;
Instruct future beneficiaries to affix identical signatures and direct the disbursing officers to verify the same.
Assistance shall only be given to the persons identified and listed. In case of a representative claimant, an authorization shall be attached. Meanwhile, in case of replacement, records shall be updated to reflect the new payee

We recommendedin October 27, 2020 that management:
Organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the 72 beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC;
Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/ status of their verification and the actions taken by management and liable persons thereon.
We extend our appreciation for the prompt response and action by management with regard to our audit observation. We have reviewed the validation report we received last December 23, 2020, which justified the receipt of seventy-two (72) recipients. Some had the following reasons:
Error in filling-up the SAC since some were not living alone
Some recipients are members of the vulnerable group but the same was not disclosed in the SAC.
Notwithstanding, we would like to humbly point out that thirty-nine (39) recipients had no valid justification if the eligibility guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 2020-09 are strictly followed.
The audit team wishes to stress that DSWD-Regional Office No. 10 is the party to the Memorandum of Agreement and the agency which promulgated the guidelines on the Emergency Subsidy Program, the regulation which serves as the legal criteria in our audit. Moreover, it is noteworthy that under Section VIII-E of DSWD Memo Circular 2020-09, the DSWD shall conduct validation within fifteen (15) days from the receipt of documents for determination of eligibility and duplication.
Accordingly, we updated our recommendation thatmanagement confer with DSWD-Regional Office No. 10 on the results of the revalidation and obtain written conformance to the justification and/or written advice as to the courses of action if recipients are declared to be ineligible. Likewise, we recommended that management update the details in the SAC which may be useful for future reference.

We have reviewed the validation report we received last December 23, 2020, which justified the receipt of twelve (12) recipientswhich reasoned among others that there were other family members listed in the SAC besides the “duplicate members.” This would translate that even if duplicate members are removed from the SAC, each recipient subsist to constitute as another family with separate head.

utilization for 20% development fund be intended for priority projects and
programs which are capital in nature in accordance with the provisions of
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommend that the Municipality Accountant correct the entry made in
recording the transactions for the reforestation and greening projects as
asset, instead of expenses. Below is the suggested entry:

Plants and Trees P710,137.00
Other Operating Expenses P710,137.00

To correct entry made in recording the expenditures for
reforestation and greening project for CY 2017.

We recommend that the Municipality comply the existing regulations for the
establishment of the LDDRM Office and fill up the mandatory positions
required to perform the functions of research & planning and operations &
warning.

We recommend that the municipality secure a final disposal site allowed by
regulatory agencies, so that residual wastes are disposed in a proper location
to protect the constituents and environment from potentially harmful
elements and to comply with the provisions of RA 9003.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the concerned
elective officials comply with the rules and regulations of the GSIS
particularly on the compulsory membership and personal monthly
contributions to be deducted from their fixed monthly salary.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that
the all the provisions of the Philippine Bidding Documents are complied,
such that all required bidding documents are issued to the bidders in the
conduct of procurement of infrastructure projects.

We recommend that the LGU submit justification on the following:
1. Why the requirement of the posting of performance security was not
imposed on the procurement of infrastructure projects.
2. Why the award of the contract was not annulled and bid security
forfeited after failure of posting of performance bond.

We recommend that BAC follow the procedures in the proper conduct of
negotiated procurement in compliance with the IRR of RA 9184, and
exercise proper diligence especially on selection of supplier with the
lowest calculated and responsive quotation.

We recommend that the LGU provide the required separate budget for the
LCPC equivalent to 1% of the IRA to guarantee implementation and
strengthening of the programs, projects and activities for the protection of
children.

We recommend that henceforth, the concerned municipal officials adhere
strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of PS limitation to
avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of Real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections;
Initiate tax campaign in the different barangays of the municipality and enforce individual targets on all revenue collection personnel and periodically monitor their accomplishments; and
Regularly prepare and submit a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the sanggunian for the latter to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to make representation with the sanggunian to promulgate an ordinance with specific guidelines on the purpose and utilization of donated rice land to safeguard government assets against losses and for proper recognition in the books of the municipality;
For purposes of lease on these donated lot properties, a written lease agreement shall be executed by and between the municipality and the farmer-lessee to protect the rights and responsibilities of both parties; and
To instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize the revenue and merchandise inventory accounts in the books of the municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management committed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the assets under the appropriate PPSAS account;
To create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of physical count of the biological assets and reconcile actual count with balances recorded in the books of the municipality; and
To instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct proper disposal of the assets and request for relief of accountability if so warrant.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to expedite implementation and completion of the projects in accordance with the guidelines stated in memorandum of agreement with source agency for timely benefit to constituents;
To instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct reconciliation of records with source agency for proper recognition of fund transfer balances and full liquidation; and
To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return to source agencies all unexpended fund balances on all completed projects, and for the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to record the return as evidenced by the source agency’s official receipt, otherwise to request for authority to realign said balances for other priority projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to secure from the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated, Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines, and League of Municipalities of the Philippines a government official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.

We recommend that the LGU through the LDRRMO ensures that the formulation and approval of the LDRRMP be made before making allocations to the LDRRMF budgeted for the year to ensure that the utilization of the Fund is in accordance with the approved financial plan of the LGU and that disbursements are in line with the basic purpose of disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Plans has been formulated and submitted for approval.

Likewise, the Municipal Mayor should demand from the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation of the cash advances granted to her, otherwise, impose appropriate sanctions and require the refund of the amount involved.
observations.managementAction: Demand for submission of documents for liquidation were made by management to municipal treasurer but she failed to submit.
observations.reason: Management decided to bring the matter into court.

observations.recommendation: The total amount disbursed should be refunded and the agency should henceforth refrain from disbursing honoraria not in accordance with Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The management made demands an answer for the transactions being done w/o public bidding.
observations.reason: The management decided to bring this matter into court.

observations.recommendation: The irregular disbursement of confidential and intelligence fund amounting to P70,000.00 should be refunded. The budget officer and the Sangguniang Bayan are reminded to follow the guidelines set in the DILG Circular as regards the disposition of confidential and intelligence funds to avoid future disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management made demands an answer for the transactions and be refunded by the responsible persons who approved and released the payment.
observations.reason: The management decided to bring this matter into court.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LGU through the LDRRMO ensures that  the  formulation
and approval of the LDRRMP be made before making allocations to the  LDRRMF
budgeted for the year to ensure that the utilization  of  the  Fund  is  in
accordance  with  the  approved  financial  plan  of  the  LGU  and    that
disbursements are in line with the basic purpose of disaster risk reduction
and management.

We recommend that henceforth, the Municipal  Accountant  judiciously  record
transactions of the Municipality to avoid errors, material or  otherwise  in
the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipality fully account  the  composition  of  Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF account in the General Fund books to ensure that only  the
unexpended balances of QRF, DRRMF-MOOE and DRRMF-CO are included therein.

We recommend that the BAC comply with GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and use  the
prescribed Annual Procurement Plan (APP) format in consolidating  the  PPMPs
of the of end-user units. Any procurement not in accordance therewith  shall
be a ground for disallowance.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20%  Development  Fund
be utilized to fund priority programs or projects which are  investments  or
capital in nature in  accordance  with  the  provisions  of  DILG-DBM  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that the LGU ensure that feasible plans will  be  adopted  and
immediately implemented to ensure compliance with the  requirements  of  RA
9003, particularly on the closure of the open dumpsite.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer comply the provisions  of  Circular
No. 0213-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of  projects  at  the
beginning of the year in proper format as well as the quarterly  information
of PPAs of the municipality.

We recommend that the persons responsible for the  procurement  immediately
comply with the requirements of  the  Notice  of  Suspension  to  avoid  the
disallowance of the questioned transactions.

We recommend that the persons responsible to comply  with  the  requirements
of the Notice of Suspension .

We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval; and
To strictly enforce the provisions of Section 16, Rule V of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the professionalization of BAC, TWG Members, and Procurement Units, whereby the HoPE shall ensure that they are sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Accountable Officers to deposit intact all their collections, as well as collections turned over to them by sub-collectors/tellers, with authorized government depository bank (AGDB) daily or not later than the next banking day; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to impose upon the revenue collectors to strictly observe daily turn-over of collections.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the capitalized transactions to the proper expense accounts so that the correct balances of the Construction in Progress – Agency Assets and the operating expenses accounts can be properly stated in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Recommendation has been implemented and proper recording was applied December 31, 2015

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfers the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2014 to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRMF” using the proper journal entries.
We recommend further, that the Municipal Accountant verifies the completeness and accuracy of the amount set up to Trust Liability-DRRM during CY 2014 and book adjustments for deficiencies / differences as necessary. A copy of this report shall be provided to COA.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant booked up the Trust Liability DRRMF in the books of the General Fund account by debiting the subsidy to other funds and credited the liability on December 2015 but the actual transfer of the fund was not yet done. The accountant also requested SB Resolution for the authority to transfer but not yet provided. On December 2016 a voucher was prepared for the transfer and at the same time opening an account exclusively for 5% DRRMF January 2017.
observations.reason: The municipal accountant booked up the Trust Liability DRRMF in the books of the General Fund account by debiting the subsidy to other funds and credited the liability on December 2015 but the actual transfer of the fund was not yet done. The accountant also requested SB Resolution for the authority to transfer but not yet provided. On December 2016 a voucher was prepared for the transfer and at the same time opening an account exclusively for 5% DRRMF January 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly enforce the proper accomplishment of the Driver's Trip Tickets by ensuring that complete information are stated in the appropriate spaces provided in the form before authorizing the use of government vehicle. Further, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of fuel Consumption in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to prove the validity of the expenses.
observations.managementAction: The municipal with the mayor, SB members and all departments concerned were advised to prepare trip tickets properly filled up for the use of fuel oil and lubricants. "No trip tickets to fuel" is the decided policy for the gasoline consumption effective January 2016.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Special Education Fund be utilized for expenses that are authorized in accordance with the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board had discussed on the proper utilization of the fund and agreed to stop the practice and to effect year 2016.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, the BAC should observe prescribed bidding procedures in all procurement, including those that involve participation of foreign suppliers. Meanwhile a written explanation be submitted on the failure to observe prescribed bidding procedures in the procurement of backhoe loader without prejudice to appropriate sanctions for failure to follow the prescribed bidding procedure.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the amount debited by the Land Bank of the Philippines on August 2014 was actually paid to the foreign supplier only on January 2015 when the equipment was delivered and inspected, thus did not violate the provision of RA 9184. Further, the BAC Chairman has submitted the written explanation citing that it was the first time they encountered such kind of transaction, and henceforth, do proper bidding practices.

observations.recommendation: In view thereof, we recommend the Municipal Mayor to issue demand and enforce the immediate settlement of the cash advance to avoid being reported to appropriate agencies for the filing of appropriate charges in accordance with COA Memorandum 2004-014 and 2005-074.
observations.managementAction: Full payment made.

observations.recommendation: In view thereof, we recommend the concerned Municipal Mayor the immediate settlement of the cash advances to avoid being reported to appropriate agencies for the filing of appropriate charges in accordance with COA Memorandum 2004-014 and 2005-074.
observations.managementAction: Fully settled cash advance. Monitoring of cash advances disclosed that as of December 31, 2016 the balance of his cash advance was only P17,500.00 and fully paid on January , 2017. .
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2016 the total unliquidated cash advances of the LGU is only P27,536.04 and fully paid on January 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accountant to enforce immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2013. Otherwise, the concerned Local Chief Executive should advice in writing the concerned accountable officers and employees to suspend the salaries of concerned accountable officers and employees to compel the settlement of their respective outstanding accounts. Also, verify thoroughly the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Proper monitoring and strict implementation and compliance of COA Rules regarding the granting of cash advances to officers and employees. Compelled officers and employees with outstanding balances of cash advance to fully settle.
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2016 the total unliquidated cash advances of the LGU is only P27,536.04 and fully paid on January 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to verify the amount payable to BIR and thereafter remit the same to BIR. Henceforth, we recommend prompt and regular remittance of all taxes withheld within the prescribed time to avoid penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2016, Due to BIR account had a balance of P158,629.55. This balance represents the December 2016 tax withheld and remitted on or before the 10th day of the following month.

observations.recommendation: The former Municipal Mayor Rogelio C. Baquerfo should be made to account for the P250,000.00.
observations.managementAction: This transaction was brought by the Office of the President to court. Series of hearings were conducted until present and one was found guilty and was dismissed from office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality comply with the rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by facilitating the closure of the open dumpsite and implementing proper handling of solid wastes. Further, the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan should be finalized and submitted to the appropriate regulatory body.
observations.managementAction: The municipality started complying with the rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of public health and environment by constructing a Material Recovery Facility (MFR) for proper handling of solid waste so that closure of open dumpsite will commence. The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was already finished and received by the proper authorities.
observations.reason: Budget for closure of open dumpsite for 2016 not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality comply with the policy on GAD otherwise known as the Magna Carta for Women, and ensure full implementation of all projects, programs and activities included in the GAD plan to achieve the overall goals of gender and development.
observations.managementAction: The municipality complied the policy on GAD budget and all applicable provisions in RA No. 9710 otherwise known as the Magna Carta for Women, and ensured all projects and programs in GAD plan should be implemented as planned.
observations.reason: Non-implementation of all GAD plans

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Municipal Mayor direct the persons responsible to submit the documents required in the Notice of Suspension so that the suspension will not mature into a disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The required document was the MOA and immediately submitted to COA team assigned in the municipality.
observations.reason: Document for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to reconcile the result of the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments thereto, preferably before the year ends so as not to reiterate this observation. Further, cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers / cards which shall be kept for each class of PPE to record the description, acquisition, transfer/disposal and other information about the asset and also submit the final physical inventory report.
observations.managementAction: The municipality had started physical count of inventory on the current year but was not completed and no report was submitted.
observations.reason: Non-completion of physical count of inventory on the current year. Subsidiary ledgers/cards per item of PPE were still not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management notify the public through signboards of their projects at the project location and adhere to the other provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The municipal engineer received an audit observation memorandum currently and promised to comply COA regulations on publicity of projects.
observations.reason: Lay out of the signboards were prepared for various projects to comply COA Circular No. 2013-04

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to R.A. 10121 dated May 27, 2010 and to the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF of LGUs, NDRRMF given to LGUs and receipts from other sources provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, thereby cause the transfer of unexpended LDRRMF to the special trust fund account.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the LDDRMF for CY 2014 was made by the Accounting and Budget Office.
observations.reason: In process of transferring unexpended LDRRMF to special trust fund account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop immediately paying employer’s share to the Fund in excess of what is required by law and instead comply strictly with the above regulations effective immediately to avoid disallowance in audit of the excessive remittance. Also, coordinate with Pag-ibig on the possible application of the over remittance of employer’s (government) share for succeeding dues on monthly contribution, otherwise, the excessive remittance shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 103 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: As of CY 2015, the practice has been stopped.
observations.reason: Previous year’s over remittance of contribution were not yet corrected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit legal basis to support the disbursement / grant of Calamity Assistance to the municipality’s regular and casual employees as well as the elected officials in the total amount of P448,000.00 to prevent disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The recipients of the calamity assistance made refunds thru payroll deductions that starting December 2016.
observations.reason: Refunds ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the management the following:
• Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 92-382 by depositing his collections regularly.
• Require all the collectors to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits as well as the inventory of accountable forms assigned to them.
• Assign a Liquidating Officer who shall perform the procedure of verifying the receipts/collections turned over by the collectors and prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits to summarize the collections turned over to him as well as his own collections.
• Provide a safety vault for the money in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer awaiting deposit.
• Conduct reconciliation of the Cash in Vault account recorded per cash book and per general ledger and effect necessary adjustment thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality currently has no Treasurer, instead, an officer in charge from the Province of Cebu. Treasury staff observant of COA rules in handling collections and deposits. Treasury staff deposited immediately on the following banking day. Due to limited budget of travel expenses, treasury staff travels at most 2 times a month to deposit collections. Also, bad weather conditions can cause delay in deposit since LGU is located in an island.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the cash in vault account had a balance of P168,778.90 of collection s but deposited immediatel y on the following banking day. Due to limited budget of travel expenses, treasury staff travels at most 2 times a month to deposit collections. Also, bad weather conditions can cause delay in deposit since LGU is located in an island.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local Chief Executive direct the officers and employees concerned to refund the amount received in excess of the allowed PEI of P5,000.00 for CY 2014.
observations.managementAction: Refunds were made by the regular employees and casual employees per recommendation by COA.
observations.reason: Refunds ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to verify the Due From and Due To Other Funds and other affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The accounts affected is for reconciliation.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the reciprocal accounts still remained unreconcil ed. As of January 31, 2017, the reciprocal accounts already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant must refrain from processing disbursement claims with supporting documents of doubtful validity such as undated and unnumbered price quotations/canvass papers, purchase requests and orders. Most importantly, the Municipality should not limit its canvassing of prices to a chosen few suppliers as it would deprive the availment of the most advantageous price of supplies and materials in the market.
observations.managementAction: Price quotations sampled and verified during audit are from supplier names not selling the materials. This can be beyond the control and knowledge of the accountant in validating the price canvass papers.

observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to send demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances, urging them to immediately liquidate/settle their unliquidated cash advances supported with complete documentation. For failure to settle their cash advances, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries.

Stop the granting of new cash advance unless the previous advances have been liquidated to avoid its accumulation.

Direct them to liquidate their cash advance upon completion of the purpose for which their cash advances were granted.

The accounting unit should closely monitor the liquidation in order to prevent undue accumulation of unsettled advances.

Management should furnish the department heads a listing of unliquidated cash advances of employees assigned to their respective sectors.

Management shall exert all efforts in locating the whereabouts of inactive personnel to pursue the settlement of the advances.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2015 | Demand letters were issued by the accountant to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances balances.  However, salaries of these personnel were not withheld during the year, thereby resulting to unliquidated balances.


Partial collections were taken from new cash advances granted to liquidate previous issuances.

Strictly enforced by management.



Monitoring by accountant was done monthly.



List was furnished to department heads regularly.



Family members of inactive members were also notified relative to cash advances balances of inactive personnel.
observations.reason: Since cash advances granted to personnel of the municipality has remained unliquidated despite efforts to collect, this observation is reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
Require the accountable officer to prepare monthly the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit the same to the municipal treasurer.

Require the municipal treasurer to prepare the consolidated Inventory and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit it to the Unit Auditor not later that the fifth day of the ensuing month as required by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2015 | Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms were prepared last July 2016.



Consolidation of these reports are still in process and soon as completed, submission to the Unit Auditor shall be done.

observations.recommendation: Require the supply officer of the General Service Office to submit to the Auditor, duly verified, approved, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travel, with supporting Driver’s Trip Tickets, in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61, which provides the rules and regulations governing the use of the government transportations thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | Personnel in charge of this report has complied with this recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concerned LGUs and NGAs to submit the following documentary requirements, where applicable, as prescribed under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Subsequently, correcting entries should be effected on the recording of funds released to the various LGUs and NGAs. Hence, the accounts Due From LGUs and Due From NGAs should be debited to record the release of funds to LGUs and NGAs for the implementation of projects/programs/activities, and credit these accounts for the liquidation of the subject funds.

Refrain from incurring unnecessary expenditure of government funds to preclude loss of wastage thereof and limit expenses to those which are essential and supportive of the implementation of the objectives and mission of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2015 | Documentary requirements have been submitted and complied with.





Liquidation reports are to be submitted for fund transfers amounting to P5,500.00 and above.  Below this amount are charged outright as donation expense.







Well taken and noted by management.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly comply with Section 29 of the general provisions of RA 10352 or 2013 GAA.

Provide at least one-percent (1%) of its total budget appropriation for programs and projects that would benefit its senior citizens and the differently-abled officers and employees.  The plan/program/project for them should be integrated in the annual regular activities of the Center.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | Per Appropriation Ordinance No. 14-06 enacted December 3, 2014 for Fiscal Year 2015 Local Expenditure Program of the General Fund, and Per Municipal Ordinance No. 15-02 enacted March 25, 2015 and approved April 8, 2015, the ordinance amending/ modifying section 6 of Appropriation Ordinance No. 14-06 on the funds intended for Senior Citizens and PWDs amounting to P 613,350.00.

observations.recommendation: Management should facilitate the adoption of Payroll-thru-bank System, which is a much safer scheme that precludes hold-ups and similar incidents involving payrolls, and promotes operational efficiency.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | Salaries of all regular personnel of the municipality were facilitated thru ATM starting January 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management that an Internal Audit Service be created and organized independent of the Accounting Department, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of R.A. No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278. Moreover, it should be required to regularly evaluate 	and/or monitor the implementation of its internal accounting and operational controls in all aspects of its operations to ensure that reliability of data, assets and records are safeguarded, and that operational efficiency and adherence to prescribed policies are promoted.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | An Internal Audit Service personnel was already designated, but still within the accounting office instead of within the municipal mayor’s office.  However, regular evaluation and monitoring of the municipality’s internal and operational controls are still being conducted with all functions of the IAUS as required with the DBM PFMAT.

observations.recommendation:
Prepare and submit its Annual GAD Plan and Budget to the NCRFW for review and endorsement to the DBM.

The GAD Focal Person shall refer to the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), National Economic and Development Auditory (NEDA), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular No. 12091, superseded by the DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 which took effect in CY 2013. This new Joint Circular provides clearer guidelines and procedures in the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of the agency annual GAD plans and budgets and GAD accomplishment reports.

Prepare the municipality's GPB during the budget preparation period as this document supports the GAD budget of a government agency.

Show the rationale on why the GAD Plans and Budget was not submitted for review to the NCRFW, NEDA and DBM as required under their Joint circular No.2004-1, which has rendered questionable the validity of GAD’s expenditures.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | Prepared and submitted the 2016 GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Board last March 22, 2016.  However, this was not endorsed by the DILG.
